cabi_bytes:
err=1
str clear=\E[H\E[2J
str cup=\E[%i%p1%d;%p2%dH
str el=\E[K
str el1=\E[1K
str cuu1=\E[A
str home=\E[H
str sgr0=\E(B\E[m
str op=\E[39;49m
str setaf=\E[3%p1%dm
str setab=\E[4%p1%dm
str bel=^7
str hpa=\E[%i%p1%dG
str vpa=\E[%i%p1%dd
num colors=8
num cols=80
num lines=24
num it=8
num pairs=64
flag am=1
flag bce=1
flag msgr=1
flag xenl=1
flag hc=0
tgoto cup 4,2=\E[3;5H
tparm cup 2,4=\E[3;5H
tparm cup 23,11=\E[24;12H
tparm setaf 1=\E[31m
tparm setab 4=\E[44m
tparm setaf 0=\E[30m
tputs clear ->\E[H\E[2J
notes: The native C-ABI staticlib, linked into an ordinary C program, returns byte-identical terminfo results to the system libtinfo for setupterm + tigetstr/tigetnum/tigetflag + tputs/tgoto + the variadic tparm(...) (ABI-VAR-01: a fixed nine-c_long definition is call-compatible with variadic callers on register-based ABIs). This is the drop-in surface (gap-ledger STRUCT-01/02): a thread-local cur_term holds the loaded entry and owns the returned strings. The curses window/screen symbols are not yet wired.
oracle_method: compile one C program twice -- linked to the native libncurses_cabi.a and to the system libtinfo -- run both under TERM=xterm and compare stdout
